message:
fix: non-blocking cluster data fetching for instant dashboard rendering (#7640)

settledWithConcurrency now accepts an onSettled callback invoked each
time a single task settles, so progressive fetchers push partial results
to the UI immediately instead of waiting for all clusters (including
unreachable ones with 15s timeouts) to complete.

- Add onSettled callback parameter to settledWithConcurrency
- Convert sequential for...of loops in progressive fetchers to concurrent
  settledWithConcurrency calls with per-task progress reporting
- Update fetchFromAllClusters, fetchPodIssuesViaAgent,
  fetchDeploymentsViaAgent, fetchWorkloadsFromAgent,
  fetchSecurityIssuesViaKubectl, fetchLLMdServers, fetchLLMdModels,
  and ISO27001 audit to use onSettled for incremental rendering

Reachable clusters now appear in <100ms while unreachable ones time out
in the background without blocking the dashboard.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
